arts.ttl: Pundits divided as Ruud van Nistelrooy looks to repeat Leicester City magic against Brighton
arts.sum: Ruud van Nistelrooy looked like he had the magic touch after guiding Leicester City to victory against West Ham just three days after watching his new team blitzed by Brentford. Harry Redknapp: “I was as shocked as anyone about Ruud van Nistelrooy managing Leicester, and the shock factor went into overdrive when Jamie Vardy pounced after 98 seconds and got that goal against West Ham. Ruud may say he’s not a romantic manager and is purely a results man, but even he would have noticed the romance there.

arts.ttl: Winks, Buonanotte, El Khannouss - Leicester injury update for Brighton clash
arts.sum: Asked if there were any new injuries in his ranks, van Nistelrooy said at his press conference on Friday afternoon: “The squad from Tuesday was available today to train. Van Nistelrooy will have to make at least one change to his 11 on Sunday afternoon though.

arts.ttl: 'The pace he has...': Van Nistelrooy really impressed by Leicester ace who could make first Prem start vs Brighton
arts.sum: He was very impressive vs West Ham...

arts.ttl: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler says 'the result will come' before Leicester Premier League clash
arts.sum: The Seagulls head coach commented on the importance of Sunday’s game before his side travel away to Leicester.

arts.ttl: Brighton on verge of beating 4 Premier League clubs to signing – Tipped to be one of league’s biggest transfers
arts.sum: Brighton and Hove Albion are currently leading the race for Hammarby youngster Bazoumana Toure, who has a host of Premier League clubs after him. Crystal Palace, Wolverhampton Wanderers, Manchester United and Arsenal are all keen. That’s according to Fotboll Direkt, who report that Brighton are the ‘current hottest track’ for the 18-year-old winger.
